message.content:
Review the techbyflo project frontend. It's a personal website + Privacy Score Quiz business.

Read these files and provide a thorough review focused on: code quality, UX/conversion issues, security, and business logic correctness.

Files to review:
- /c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx
- /c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/home.tsx
- /c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/work-with-me.tsx
- /c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/data/quiz-questions.ts
- /c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/data/offers.ts
- /c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/App.tsx

For each file note:
1. Code quality issues (bugs, antipatterns, TypeScript errors)
2. Business logic issues (quiz scoring, offers, upsell flow)
3. UX/conversion issues
4. Missing features that would help sell services

Return a structured report with CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM issues.

content.text: I'll read all the files in parallel to conduct a thorough review.

1 import Layout from "@/components/layout"; 2 import { Link } from "wouter"; 3 import { 4 Shield, Settings, Brain, ArrowRight, AlertTriangle, 5 Lock, ChevronRight, 6 } from "lucide-react"; 7 8 import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react"; 9 10 // ── Typewriter hook ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 11 function useTypewriter(texts: string[], speed = 75, pause = 2200) { 12 const [state, setState] = useState({ textIndex: 0, charIndex: 0, deleting: false }); 13 14 useEffect(() => { 15 const { textIndex, charIndex, deleting } = state; 16 const current = texts[textIndex]; 17 let delay: number; 18 19 if (!deleting && charIndex < current.length) { 20 delay = speed; 21 const t = setTimeout(() => setState(s => ({ ...s, charIndex: s.charIndex + 1 })), delay); 22 return () => clearTimeout(t); 23 } 24 if (!deleting && charIndex === current.length) { 25 delay = pause; 26 const t = setTimeout(() => setState(s => ({ ...s, deleting: true })), delay); 27 return () => clearTimeout(t); 28 } 29 if (deleting && charIndex > 0) { 30 delay = speed / 2.2; 31 const t = setTimeout(() => setState(s => ({ ...s, charIndex: s.charIndex - 1 })), delay); 32 return () => clearTimeout(t); 33 } 34 const t = setTimeout(() => 35 setState(s => ({ deleting: false, charIndex: 0, textIndex: (s.textIndex + 1) % texts.length })), 36 160 37 ); 38 return () => clearTimeout(t); 39 }, [state, texts, speed, pause]); 40 41 return texts[state.textIndex].slice(0, state.charIndex); 42 } 43 44 // ── Floating particles ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 45 function ParticleBackground() { 46 const particles = useMemo(() => 47 Array.from({ length: 18 }, (_, i) => ({ 48 id: i, 49 left: 5 + Math.random() * 90, 50 size: 2 + Math.random() * 3.5, 51 delay: Math.random() * 12, 52 duration: 10 + Math.random() * 14, 53 })), [] 54 ); 55 56 return ( 57

Está pensado precisamente para gente normal: profesionales, familias y creadores que quieren estar protegidos sin volverse expertos. Yo traduzco lo técnico a pasos claros para que sepas qué hacer, en qué orden y con criterio.", 175 }, 176 { 177 question: "¿Qué incluye exactamente Blindaje Digital Personal?", 178 answer: 179 "Incluye una hoja de ruta práctica para cerrar tus riesgos más importantes: cuentas, contraseñas, verificación en dos pasos, dispositivos y hábitos clave. Recibes un plan accionable, sin relleno, para que pases de la duda a tener tu seguridad bajo control.", 180 }, 181 { 182 question: "¿Diferencia entre base y Plus?", 183 answer: 184 "La versión base te da estructura y pasos concretos para ejecutarlo por tu cuenta. La versión Plus añade acompañamiento directo conmigo, revisión personalizada y soporte para resolver bloqueos en tiempo real. Mismo método, distinto nivel de profundidad y acompañamiento.", 185 }, 186 { 187 question: "¿Sirve para autónomos/pequeños negocios?", 188 answer: 189 "Totalmente. De hecho, es de los perfiles que más se beneficia porque suele tener mucha exposición digital y poco tiempo. Adaptamos prioridades para proteger clientes, cuentas, cobros y reputación sin complicar la operativa diaria.", 190 }, 191 { 192 question: "¿Trabajas con equipos o empresas?", 193 answer: 194 "Sí. Trabajo con equipos y empresas en formato formación, consultoría o colaboración continua. Si tu caso es corporativo, diseñamos una propuesta a medida para vuestro nivel técnico, sector y objetivos de negocio.", 195 }, 196 { 197 question: "¿Necesito cambiar herramientas actuales?", 198 answer: 199 "No necesariamente. Primero optimizamos lo que ya usas y solo recomendamos cambios cuando hay una mejora real en seguridad o simplicidad. El objetivo es protegerte mejor, no llenarte de apps.", 200 }, 201 { 202 question: "¿Ofreces seguimiento mensual?", 203 answer: 204 "Sí, en formato de seguimiento para quienes quieren mantener su blindaje al día. 183	export type ScoreCategory = {

185	  emoji: string;
186	  color: string;

188	  bgColor: string;
189	  borderColor: string;
190	  percentile: string;
191	  description: string;

194	export function getScoreCategory(score: number): ScoreCategory {
195	  if (score >= 81) return {
196	    label: "Blindado",
197	    emoji: "🛡️",
198	    color: "green",
199	    textColor: "text-green-400",
200	    bgColor: "bg-green-500/10",
201	    borderColor: "border-green-500/30",
202	    percentile: "estás entre el 10% más protegidos",
203	    description: "Tienes hábitos de seguridad digital excepcionales. Estás entre el 10% mejor protegidos. Pero la seguridad no es un destino — es un hábito. Aquí tienes lo que te falta para mantenerte en el nivel más alto.",
204	  };
205	  if (score >= 61) return {
206	    label: "Protegido",
207	    emoji: "🔵",
208	    color: "blue",
209	    textColor: "text-blue-400",
210	    bgColor: "bg-blue-500/10",
211	    borderColor: "border-blue-500/30",
212	    percentile: "mejor que el 65% de usuarios",
213	    description: "Buena base, pero hay brechas que un atacante con motivación podría aprovechar. El informe completo te dice exactamente qué cerrar primero para pasar al nivel Blindado.",
214	  };
215	  if (score >= 41) return {
216	    label: "En Riesgo",
217	    emoji: "🟡",
218	    color: "yellow",
219	    textColor: "text-yellow-400",
220	    bgColor: "bg-yellow-500/10",
221	    borderColor: "border-yellow-500/30",
222	    percentile: "mejor que el 40% de usuarios",
223	    description: "Tienes la base, pero hay brechas que cualquier estafador puede aprovechar. No es cuestión de SI te van a atacar — es de cuándo. El Kit Escudo te da el plan exacto para cerrarlas.",
224	  };
225	  if (score >= 21) return {
226	    label: "Vulnerable",
227	    emoji: "🟠",
228	    color: "orange",
229	    textColor: "text-orange-400",
230	    bgColor: "bg-orange-500/10",
231	    borderColor: "border-orange-500/30",
232	    percentile: "en riesgo mayor que el 75% de usuarios",
233	    description: "Estás expuesto a ataques comunes HOY. Estafas de phishing, robo de cuentas o Bizum inverso no son hipótesis — son escenarios probables con tu nivel actual. Necesitas un plan de acción urgente.",
234	  };
235	  return {
236	    label: "Crítico",
237	    emoji: "🔴",
238	    color: "red",
239	    textColor: "text-red-400",
240	    bgColor: "bg-red-500/10",
241	    borderColor: "border-red-500/30",
242	    percentile: "en el 5% de mayor riesgo",
243	    description: "Tu seguridad digital está en rojo. Contraseñas reutilizadas, sin 2FA, sin backups — cualquier phishing básico puede vaciarte las cuentas AHORA MISMO. Necesitas actuar hoy, no mañana.",
244	  };
245	}
246	
247	export function calculateScore(answers: number[]): number {
248	  const total = answers.reduce((acc, pts) => acc + pts, 0);
249	  const maxPossible = 7 * QUIZ_QUESTIONS.length;
250	  return Math.round((total / maxPossible) * 100);
251	}

content.text: Now let me check a few additional details about the checkout URL construction and routing.

I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Here is the full structured review. --- # TechByFlo Frontend — Code Review Report --- ## CRITICAL --- ### [CRITICAL-1] App.tsx renders only the Maintenance page — the entire site is unreachable **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/App.tsx` The router is completely bypassed. Every URL renders ``. The quiz funnel, home page, and all commercial pages are dead to any live visitor. This is the single highest-impact issue in the project. ```tsx // Current — all routes dead function App() { return ( // <-- hardcoded, no Switch/Route ); } ``` The CLAUDE.md explicitly notes this: "To restore normal routing, replace the `` render with the full `` route tree." This needs a `` with all 11 routes before the site can generate any revenue. --- ### [CRITICAL-2] Email validation is trivially bypassable — any garbage email enters the CRM **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, line 210 ```tsx if (!email.includes("@") || !email.includes(".")) { setError("Introduce un email válido"); return; } ``` This passes `a@b.` or `@@.` as valid. The `` browser validation is already there and more robust. The manual check adds false confidence without adding safety. The bigger risk: invalid or bot-submitted emails pollute the Resend audience and degrade deliverability. The project already has Zod installed — this is the correct fix: ```tsx import { z } from "zod"; const emailSchema = z.string().email(); // In handleSubmit: const result = emailSchema.safeParse(email); if (!result.success) { setError("Introduce un email válido"); return; } ``` --- ### [CRITICAL-3] Server-side email input is not validated — `/api/subscribe` trusts raw body **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/subscribe.ts`, lines 143–147 ```ts const { email, score } = req.body as { email?: string; score?: number }; if (!email || typeof score !== "number") { ... } ``` There is no format check on `email` before it is passed to `resend.contacts.create()` and `resend.emails.send()`. A crafted POST with `email: "not-an-email"` or `score: -999` will silently call the Resend API with garbage data. Because the endpoint always returns HTTP 200, the caller never knows whether the email was malformed. Add Zod validation server-side: ```ts import { z } from "zod"; const bodySchema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), score: z.number().int().min(0).max(100), }); const parsed = bodySchema.safeParse(req.body); if (!parsed.success) return res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid input" }); ``` --- ### [CRITICAL-4] Score injection — client-supplied score is trusted without server-side recalculation **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/subscribe.ts`, line 143 The score sent in the POST body is whatever the client sends. There is no recalculation from answers on the server. A user can POST `{ email: "real@email.com", score: 5 }` to receive the "Crítico" email with a CTA for the 29€ Kit Escudo, or `{ score: 100 }` to get the "Blindado" message. This can be exploited to manipulate which upsell email is delivered. Since answers are not sent to the server, at minimum the score should be clamped to `[0, 100]` and validated as an integer. --- ## HIGH --- ### [HIGH-1] Progress bar reports 0% on the first question — creates a broken first impression **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, line 118 ```tsx const progress = ((questionIndex) / total) * 100; ``` When `questionIndex` is `0`, progress renders as `"0% completado"` even though the user is actively answering question 1. This looks broken. The label below says "Pregunta 1 de 15" while the bar shows 0%. The conventional fix is to count the current question as in-progress: ```tsx const progress = ((questionIndex + 1) / total) * 100; ``` --- ### [HIGH-2] Checkout URLs fall back silently to `"#"` with no user-visible warning **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, lines 431, 472; `work-with-me.tsx`, lines 21–22 ```tsx const PLAN_BASIC_URL = import.meta.env.VITE_LS_BASIC_URL ?? "#"; ``` When `VITE_LS_BASIC_URL` is not set (missing `.env` in production), the buy buttons silently become `href="#"`. The page looks live and clickable but clicking the primary CTA does nothing. There is no build-time warning or runtime guard. This is a silent revenue leak. In development this should throw; in production it should at least log a warning and render the button as disabled. --- ### [HIGH-3] Product descriptions are mismatched between pages — damages trust **File:** `work-with-me.tsx` (INDIVIDUAL_PLANS array, lines 88–98) vs. `quiz.tsx` (UpsellScreen, lines 418–427) The "Blindaje Personal" plan has two different descriptions across the two pages: - In `work-with-me.tsx` it is described as an iPhone privacy configuration guide ("Configuración completa de privacidad y seguridad para iPhone"). - In `quiz.tsx` it is described as "Tu plan de acción para las 5 vulnerabilidades más críticas de tu score" with a checklist and community access. - The CTA in `work-with-me.tsx` reads "Quiero Privacidad iPhone Pro", but the product name is "Blindaje Personal". A user who sees the quiz upsell and then visits `/trabaja-conmigo` will see a different product with the same name and price. This creates confusion and erodes trust at the purchase moment. --- ### [HIGH-4] Home page product ladder shows hardcoded prices that diverge from `offers.ts` **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/home.tsx`, lines 226–290 The home page product section hardcodes prices inline (`39€`, `49€`, `119€`, `497€`). These do not read from `OFFERS` in `offers.ts`. When a price changes in `offers.ts`, the home page remains stale. The "Blindaje Personal" card on the home page shows `39€` and `E2` tier; `offers.ts` also shows `39€` for the same product, but "Kit Escudo Digital" is listed as `E1` at `39€` — the same price for what appears to be a different product. This needs a single source of truth. --- ### [HIGH-5] The contact form in `work-with-me.tsx` falls back silently to `"#"` when `VITE_FORMSPREE_URL` is unset **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/work-with-me.tsx`, line 23 ```tsx const FORMSPREE = import.meta.env.VITE_FORMSPREE_URL ?? "#"; ``` The form submits a `fetch` to `"#"` when the env var is missing. In that case `fetch("#", { method: "POST" })` will either throw a network error or submit to the current page. The `catch` block sets `status = "error"` and shows an Instagram handle — but this is a silent misconfiguration that will lose every B2B lead in production if the variable is not set. This is a direct revenue risk for the high-ticket brand services. --- ### [HIGH-6] Email in `subscribe.ts` promotes "Kit Escudo — 29€" but the product is listed at 39€ **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/subscribe.ts`, line 109 ```ts

Kit Escudo — 29€

``` The post-quiz email sent to every subscriber advertises 29€, but all UI pages show 39€ for the same product. Every email sent to a Crítico/Vulnerable/En Riesgo user contains a price that contradicts the checkout page. This is a legal and trust issue, and will cause confusion at purchase. --- ### [HIGH-7] `useTypewriter` in `home.tsx` has an unstable dependency — `texts` array recreated on every render **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/home.tsx`, lines 88–93 ```tsx const typed = useTypewriter([ "phishing por WhatsApp.", ... ]); ``` The array literal is created inline every render. Inside `useTypewriter`, the `useEffect` depends on `texts`, so it fires on every re-render of `Home`, restarting the animation. This is benign if `Home` rarely re-renders, but it is an unstable dependency pattern. The array should be defined as a constant outside the component. --- ### [HIGH-8] `navigate` from `useLocation` is imported but never used in `quiz.tsx` **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, line 503 ```tsx const [, navigate] = useLocation(); ``` `navigate` is destructured but never called in the component. This is dead code that suggests an incomplete feature (likely the planned redirect after upsell). It should either be implemented or removed. --- ## MEDIUM --- ### [MEDIUM-1] Weak-areas logic in `ResultScreen` filters on `points < 7` but answers array stores total points per question **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, lines 316–320 ```tsx const weakAreas = QUIZ_QUESTIONS .map((q, i) => ({ q, points: answers[i] ?? 0 })) .filter(({ points }) => points < 7) ``` A question answered with the best option scores exactly 7 points. The threshold `< 7` correctly excludes perfect answers. However, a user who answered every question correctly (all 7s) would have `weakAreas = []`, and the "Tus áreas de mejora detectadas" section would render an empty list — with no fallback message. This needs a guard for the empty state. --- ### [MEDIUM-2] Blurred paywall teaser in `ResultScreen` reveals actual tip text to anyone who inspects the DOM **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, lines 362–368 ```tsx
{QUIZ_QUESTIONS.slice(3, 6).map((q) => (

{q.tip}

))}
``` The tips are fully rendered in the DOM — only CSS blur conceals them. Opening DevTools reveals the complete tip text. If this content is meant to be gated, it must not be present in the initial HTML. For a teaser this is acceptable, but it should not be presented as "locked" content if the data is in the bundle. --- ### [MEDIUM-3] The `handleRetake` function does not reset `stage` before resetting other state — no practical bug, but state order is fragile **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, lines 542–548 All state is reset in a single event handler with sequential `setState` calls. Because React 18 batches these in event handlers, this is fine in practice. However, `score` is reset to `0` after `setStage("intro")`, meaning a brief window where `stage = "intro"` but `score` still holds the previous value exists — irrelevant now, but fragile if stage renders score. --- ### [MEDIUM-4] `handleEmailSubmit` stores the email in `localStorage` without documentation or cleanup **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/quiz.tsx`, lines 534–539 ```tsx function handleEmailSubmit(_email: string) { try { localStorage.setItem("quiz_email", _email); } catch {} setStage("result"); } ``` The comment says "Placeholder: store email in localStorage for now". This persists the user's email indefinitely in `localStorage` with no TTL, no reference to where it is read back, and no cleanup. Under GDPR (applicable in Spain), storing personal data client-side without a clear purpose and retention policy is a compliance risk. Either implement the intended use or remove the storage call. --- ### [MEDIUM-5] `work-with-me.tsx` individual CTA button links to itself **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/work-with-me.tsx`, lines 684–689 ```tsx {audience === "individual" ? ( Ver planes ``` The final CTA button at the bottom of the "individual" view on `/trabaja-conmigo` links back to `/trabaja-conmigo`. The user is already on this page. The CTA either should scroll to the plans section or navigate to `/quiz` to drive the funnel. --- ### [MEDIUM-6] `UPSELL_BY_PURCHASE` map in `offers.ts` is defined but never consumed **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/data/offers.ts`, lines 78–81 ```ts export const UPSELL_BY_PURCHASE: Record = { "blindaje-personal": "borrado-huella-digital", "blindaje-personal-plus": "guardia-digital", }; ``` This upsell routing map is exported but there is no import of it anywhere in the codebase. The post-purchase upsell flow (e.g. on `/gracias`) appears to be planned but not implemented. This represents lost revenue at the highest-intent moment (right after a purchase). --- ### [MEDIUM-7] Score logic duplication between client and server without a shared contract **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/api/subscribe.ts`, lines 10–16; `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/data/quiz-questions.ts`, lines 194–245 The score-to-category mapping is duplicated in full. The comment in `subscribe.ts` acknowledges this: "duplicated from client — serverless can't import client code." The thresholds are currently in sync, but any future change to category boundaries must be made in both files separately. A shared `shared/` module (which already exists in the project) or a JSON config file should be the single source of truth. --- ### [MEDIUM-8] Home page CTA "Sin registro · Resultado inmediato" is misleading **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/home.tsx`, line 385 The subtext under the final CTA says "Sin registro · Resultado inmediato" but the quiz includes an email gate that blocks access to the result. A user clicking expecting no registration will encounter the gate and feel deceived. Either remove the claim or change it to something accurate like "Sin pago · Resultado personalizado". --- ## LOW --- ### [LOW-1] `badge: null` repeated across all but one entry in `MARCA_SERVICES` **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/pages/work-with-me.tsx`, lines 131–168 `badge: null` is explicitly set on 5 of 6 entries. The type should either make `badge` optional (`badge?: string`) to match the actual usage pattern, or the null entries should simply omit the field. --- ### [LOW-2] Array index used as `key` in multiple list renders **Files:** `quiz.tsx` lines 149, 422, 463; `work-with-me.tsx` line 482 Several lists use `key={i}` where stable keys are available (e.g. `q.id` on quiz options, `plan.label`, `pain.emoji`). This is low risk since none of these lists reorder, but it is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which generally uses stable keys. --- ### [LOW-3] `getPublicEntryOffers` and `getCoreOffers` are identical backward-compat aliases pointing to the same data **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/data/offers.ts`, lines 71–74 Both functions return `PUBLIC_OFFERS`. This is stated as backward-compat but creates confusion about which to use. If nothing imports them (confirm with a grep), they should be removed. --- ### [LOW-4] Questions 5 and 14 share the same emoji **File:** `/c/Users/flori/src/techbyflo/client/src/data/quiz-questions.ts`, lines 63, 163 Both question 5 ("¿Cuándo actualizas el sistema operativo…") and question 14 ("¿Con qué frecuencia revisas la configuración de privacidad…") use `emoji: "⚙️"`. Minor visual duplication in the quiz UI. --- ## Review Summary | Severity | Count | Status | |----------|-------|--------| | CRITICAL | 4 | block | | HIGH | 8 | warn | | MEDIUM | 8 | info | | LOW | 4 | note | **Verdict: BLOCK — 4 CRITICAL issues must be fixed before any traffic is sent to this site.** The most urgent fix is CRITICAL-1: the router override in `App.tsx` means the entire commercial funnel is unreachable by real users. CRITICAL-2 and CRITICAL-3 together mean every email entering the Resend CRM is unvalidated at both the client and server boundary. CRITICAL-4 means the post-quiz email CTA can be gamed by anyone who can send a POST request. After those four are resolved, the HIGH-priority issues — particularly the checkout URL fallback, the price discrepancy in the email, and the product description mismatch — should be addressed before running any paid traffic.
